name: financial_services_water_allocation_model_cedar_car_fair
premise: The board wants one accountable decision that can be explained simply and audited later. At Cedar Point Market Conduct & Integrity Office, a financial services unit is forced into a time-bound decision after 6 related incidents in the last 6 hours. The trigger is that a water allocation model reallocates flows away from treaty-protected fisheries during a drought, as an appeals backlog grows and frontline staff begin creating unofficial workarounds. The board has pre-approved up to $2.5M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. The moral tension is care vs fairness: a fast fix could reduce near-term harm, but it may erode safeguards that matter when errors are hardest to contest. Health Director Priya Nand calls an emergency review; logs are incomplete and staff accounts disagree with the vendor’s incident summary. Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks argues that any plan must include a credible appeals channel and a way to detect uneven error rates before they harden into routine.

agents.name: Health Director Priya Nand
agents.identity: I am Health Director Priya Nand, the accountable executive for Cedar Point Market Conduct & Integrity Office. I must translate incomplete telemetry into a defensible plan that frontline teams can execute without improvising unsafe shortcuts.
agents.goal: I want a path that stabilizes outcomes without baking in an exception that will be applied unevenly across neighborhoods and shifts harm onto people who cannot appeal quickly.

agents.name: Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks
agents.identity: I am Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks, tasked with representing staff and public stakeholders around Cedar Point Market Conduct & Integrity Office's response. I push for contestability because errors will not be evenly distributed once defaults lock in.
agents.goal: I want explicit protections for those least able to recover from a wrong guess, even if that slows deployment or forces uncomfortable transparency in the short term.

choices: I will pursue a staged intervention: cap scope, require documented criteria and a public incident dashboard, and expand only after verification, accepting slower relief if it buys legitimacy.
I will pursue an accelerated intervention across the affected footprint with daily executive review and public dashboards, accepting higher overreach risk to stop cascading failures now.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The trade space is ugly: either move fast on the intervention or build guardrails first. Health Director Priya Nand must commit. What does Health Director Priya Nand do?
